229813.explanation: The property tax (OZB) for non-residential properties (businesses) in Coevorden is well below the Drenthe average. It may increase so that our residents (homes) no longer pay disproportionately. Our residents are not a cash cow. For the OZB on homes, only inflation correction applies.

229815.explanation: PAC primarily wants to focus on the redevelopment and improvement of existing industrial parks. Business parks do not necessarily have to be located in Coevorden for our residents to benefit.

229816.explanation: PAC opposes the 'concreting' of our environment. Distribution centers contribute hardly to employment and the local economy. PAC wants to prevent the waste of valuable land. It can then be used for other purposes.

229817.explanation: PAC opposes the hardening of asylum policies and advocates for balanced distribution and humane reception. This includes investing in integration. The sooner people participate, the stronger society becomes.

229818.explanation: Information about the effects of using fireplaces and wood stoves. PAC wants a local burning ban on 'unhealthy days' such as during fog or low wind. Fireplaces and wood stoves pollute the air and lead to structural health risks.

229820.explanation: The 'Solar Ladder' is followed, and solar parks on agricultural land remain a last option: after solar on roofs, after solar on businesses, and after small-scale setups on farms.

229821.explanation: PAC finds the statement too general. We want to maintain the 'space for space' arrangement. This gives farmers the option to demolish unsightly sheds and thereby create space for housing, whether or not under their own management.

229822.explanation: PAC wants the Integral Housing Plan (IHP) to be followed. The IHP determines which schools will be renewed, renovated, or investigated over the next 15 years. PAC is positive about investments to enable inclusive education and care.

229824.explanation: Increasing the feeling of safety must primarily be promoted within society. By helping each other, we prevent vulnerable people from being left out. Cooperation between residents, the municipality, and businesses strengthens the community and thus makes it safer. More enforcement officers is not the solution.

229825.explanation: The safety of emergency responders, including enforcement officers (boa’s), is under threat. A baton provides a means to create distance when a situation escalates and prevents enforcement officers from getting injured in incidents.

229826.explanation: PAC wants new construction to be attractive not only to affluent buyers and tenants but also to offer opportunities for local starters, newcomers, movers, and renters. PAC wants at least 30% social rental housing in new building projects.

229827.explanation: PAC supports the 'owner-occupancy obligation.' This makes homes available to people who genuinely want to live there and prevents investors from making profits with excessively high rents.

229829.explanation: PAC believes there are enough parking spaces. PAC’s initiative to expand the 'Blue zone' parking in the city center is being implemented. This creates more 'flexible' parking space.

229831.explanation: PAC wants more charging points for electric cars, where strategic choices are made about the numbers and desired locations in neighborhoods, public transport hubs, and industrial estates. PAC wants these to be accessible and affordable.

229832.explanation: PAC sees shared mobility as one of the solutions to make transportation smarter, cheaper, and greener. With shared cars, bikes, and scooters combined with public transport at mobility hubs, we can move towards seeing mobility more as a service rather than ownership.

229833.explanation: PAC wants the speed limit in residential areas reduced to 30 km/h for more traffic safety, livability, and less emissions. PAC wants 60 km/h to be the norm on municipal roads outside built-up areas.

229836.explanation: PAC finds it important that homes fit the surroundings and contribute to spatial quality. The Environmental Act has already relaxed some rules. A Visual Quality Plan is helpful in providing guidelines on appearance and quality.

229837.explanation: PAC supports an owner-occupancy obligation. With pressure on the housing market, PAC finds it sensible for the municipality to develop policies on issues like private rental, protection against buy-to-let investors, and rules for room rentals. A rental register and checks on rental quality to tackle abuses.

229838.explanation: PAC is in favor of good enforcement of the 'Good Landlord Act.' Intervening in unreasonably high rents and misconduct by landlords requires local policies. The municipality itself takes responsibility for the maintenance of caravan sites.

229839.explanation: PAC supports a coherent housing vision as guidance for current and future housing needs. This is about more than just homes; it also includes livability, amenities, care, and future-proofing. PAC believes residents must be actively involved.

229841.explanation: In 10 years, the majority of households will consist of one or two persons. Building large homes is nonsense. PAC especially wants to promote the flow of elderly people, often living in large family homes, to suitable housing. This will free up large houses.

229842.explanation: PAC believes that residential zones, schools, and housing determine the pesticide spray zones, not the other way around. The use of agricultural pesticides is banned within 50 meters of food crops and 250 meters of ornamental and lily cultivation near existing or new buildings.

229843.explanation: PAC wants to reduce classification and environmental categories to a maximum of category 3.2 in industrial areas. PAC wants the municipality to encourage relocation of polluting industries (noise, odor, and environmental nuisance) to designated places.

229845.explanation: Current guidelines on 'problem wolves' already provide sufficient scope to act. PAC wants the municipality to focus on how our residents learn to coexist with the wolf. This also requires attention for animal owners who feel threatened in their livelihood.

229847.explanation: PAC sees shared mobility as one of the solutions to make transportation smarter, cheaper, and greener. To stimulate this, designating parking spaces for shared cars can be a solution.

229848.explanation: PAC believes that good and affordable transportation helps people participate. The 'doe-mee-pas' arrangement is limited. PAC wants research into the need for such a scheme and how it can best be implemented.

229849.explanation: PAC sees no necessity for this. The area has high landscape value and is an important nature reserve. With the arrival of the Nedersaksenlijn, we hope this will boost reducing car use. Moreover, most of it lies in the province of Overijssel.

229850.explanation: PAC wants to lower parking standards. The current standards cause homes to be more expensive and reserve too much scarce space for cars. PAC wants a norm of one parking space per dwelling unit.

229851.explanation: PAC believes that initiatives for new solar parks should only be permitted if the proceeds benefit the residents. PAC wants energy projects to be developed and managed locally as much as possible through cooperatives.

229852.explanation: PAC wants to counter recreational misuse with appropriate regulations. Increasing the 'commuter tax' can be used to discourage owning a 'cottage in the countryside.'

229853.explanation: PAC wants a subsidy for retail to make shop entrances more accessible for wheelchair users.

229854.explanation: PAC believes that the 'respite care in the WMO' scheme must remain. If this means more funding is needed because demand increases, PAC is positive about that. After all, an admission for a stay costs much more money, not to mention availability.

229855.explanation: PAC especially wants the municipality to encourage care providers to deploy care technology. This allows our residents to live longer or permanently in their own environment. PAC also wants the municipality to consider using care technology in public spaces, for example on the theme 'dementia-friendly.'

229856.explanation: PAC focuses on sustainable youth care, including education and prevention, so that everyone has equal opportunities. Youth consultants can be part of this. But also the knowledge and strength of parents among themselves can contribute (experiential expertise).

229859.explanation: PAC wants the participation pass to also be usable for vet visits or pet shops. People with low incomes have a higher risk of stress and isolation. Pets are often extra important. Pets help reduce stress; they provide comfort and peace.

229861.explanation: PAC is an advocate of inclusive personnel policy. Besides having a social responsibility, a diverse personnel policy also contributes additional knowledge and expertise within the organization. This also applies to employees with a disability.

229862.explanation: PAC wants people who own their own homes and want to make them more sustainable but lack the funds to borrow money from the municipality. In addition, PAC focuses on a practical and social approach with Fixteams, insulation assistance, and smart use of available national resources.

229863.explanation: Where it concerns simple questions and extending services outside office hours, PAC sees opportunities. At the same time, there are risks of unintentional GDPR violations or loss of trust because AI does not recognize the context or nuances of the question.

229865.explanation: PAC would like to experiment with different forms such as citizen panels and digital platforms. PAC wants the municipality to record how and in what way participation is organized. This strengthens trust in public administration.

229868.explanation: PAC believes the municipality of Coevorden must implement the 'statutory housing obligation for status holders.' The municipality is currently behind on this. PAC wants agreements made with housing corporations about this.

229869.explanation: Coevorden already fully complies with the distribution law, so expanding the asylum seekers' center is not obvious. However, national and international circumstances may change, making flexibility desirable here.

229870.explanation: PAC supports a small cultural space in the city of Coevorden as part of a multifunctional facility. The library can also be housed there, as well as citizens, entrepreneurs, and organizations in a cultural incubator 'Growing Coevorden.' This can be in collaboration with Theater Hofpoort.

229871.explanation: The decision on this has been made. PAC supports the further development of Holwert-Midden. The highest priority is the construction of De Nieuwe Veste.

229873.explanation: PAC finds it important that the municipality, in consultation with users/associations, focuses on retaining and improving sports fields. This may mean that some sports fields disappear and other functions replace them.

229813.explanation: We find it understandable that the property tax (OZB) is increased via indexation to account for inflation.

229815.explanation: We want to make small business parks (Dalen, Sleen, Geesbrug or Zweeloo) future-proof again.

229816.explanation: The rural area now has beautiful vistas and they want to keep it that way. Space for this could be on the industrial area in Coevorden, but not in or near the villages.

229817.explanation: Good integration is necessary for good living and working together. We want newcomers to find work as quickly as possible so they can participate in society.

229818.explanation: Fireplaces and wood stoves have often been part of houses and farms for decades. A good stove and good burning behavior usually causes no problems. Also, a ban is unenforceable.

229820.explanation: Coevorden has achieved its ambition with 100 hectares of solar panels on agricultural land. This is sufficient. We want to focus on solar on roofs at businesses and in suitable places without using good land for this.

229821.explanation: We have enabled the policy 'Flex on your own yard,' which especially offers opportunities for farmers.

229822.explanation: Not in the coming council period, but in the longer term. This is also described in the IHP.

229824.explanation: People must feel safe, so more enforcement is needed. Also because of the large area of our municipality.

229825.explanation: Our enforcers must be well-equipped according to legal regulations and must be able to intervene effectively when necessary.

229826.explanation: Making sharper (performance) agreements with housing corporations about building social rental homes and affordable owner-occupied homes, or selling homes to people with a local connection, is important to us.

229827.explanation: We want to prevent homes being bought up by commercial parties and subsequently rented out. Renting out a home should only be allowed under conditions.

229829.explanation: Coevorden currently has a 20% surplus (parking spaces). However, focus must be on sufficient handicapped parking spaces.

229831.explanation: There are more and more electric cars. That is good for the environment, but more charging points will also be needed. As much as possible in consultation with residents.

229832.explanation: Accessibility of villages and cities is very important to many people. Sometimes existing transport companies cannot deliver this, so we need to investigate how it can be done.

229833.explanation: Where necessary, 30 km/h; where possible, 50 km/h.

229836.explanation: How a house looks must fit the environment, the street, or the village where it is built. Without rules, many streets become messy.

229837.explanation: People sometimes rent out their house when they are on vacation or for other reasons; this has never caused problems so far.

229838.explanation: Good supervision is important to prevent abuses.

229839.explanation: We want to talk with all villages and residents about the need for new homes or revitalizing old housing plans.

229841.explanation: There are plenty of large and expensive houses. At this moment, it is harder for people with a normal income to find a home. That is why we want homes built specifically for young people, starters, and families.

229842.explanation: And in addition, introduce larger pesticide-free zones.

229843.explanation: Employment is important but we are against every form of industry. A healthy physical living environment is paramount. We focus on smart manufacturing industry and logistics.

229845.explanation: We support active management of the wolf. Unfortunately, this sometimes means that culling is necessary.

229847.explanation: In practice, we see that shared cars are hardly used. Therefore, more parking spaces are not necessary.

229848.explanation: Free public transport for people with low income should be open for discussion. Accessibility and livability for residents with low income must be possible. But state support is necessary for this. Since we do not expect such support, it is not feasible for the municipality.

229849.explanation: It has been shown that accidents occur regularly on the N34, so if the province wants to finance improvements, we welcome this.

229850.explanation: For new construction developments, we apply the national CROW regulation. The car is an important means of transport for our residents, and if necessary, customized solutions are possible.

229851.explanation: In the past, we have too often seen that the profits from wind and solar parks did not go to the residents of an area but to commercial developers. The surroundings were left with all the burdens. The proceeds from energy projects should also benefit the residents of villages and neighborhoods.

229852.explanation: A house is primarily a basic facility to live in. Houses should not be used for improper purposes, so we find it understandable that people who own a second home to build wealth are taxed extra for it.

229853.explanation: The surroundings of shops should be cared for, but the shop entrance is the responsibility of the entrepreneur.

229854.explanation: Caregivers should certainly be relieved, but not in this way.

229855.explanation: Due to the increasing aging population and growing shortage of healthcare personnel, this is a necessary development to be able to provide optimal care.

229856.explanation: One consultant per school is not necessary. Grouping of schools should be possible.

229859.explanation: We aim to ensure that people with financial problems are made aware of the debt counseling agency or other financial aid funds and schemes.

229861.explanation: More attention should be given to people's appropriate possibilities.

229862.explanation: Residents can already make use of various subsidy schemes.

229863.explanation: Yes, provided that people who are less proficient in the language can have personal contact.

229865.explanation: Personal contact is our top priority. Seek connection with each other.

229868.explanation: Given the tight housing market, more target groups should be given priority, and the solution to the housing crisis is simply building more homes. At the same time, currently only 1 in 20 rental homes is assigned to a refugee with a residence permit.

229869.explanation: Coevorden has been hosting a larger share of refugees than other municipalities in the Netherlands for years. We find this explainable, but it is time for other municipalities to also take their responsibility. Therefore, in principle, we are not in favor of expanding refugee reception.

229870.explanation: We must be careful with culture, but we also look at the cultural initiatives and institutions in the villages that we want to support.

229871.explanation: We have always advocated building on the current location because it is much cheaper.

229873.explanation: We support sports and outdoor sports, but if there is insufficient interest, we can jointly consider a new destination for sports fields.

229813.explanation: If you do not increase the property tax (ozb), you put the finances under such pressure that you will have to cut spending.

229815.explanation: Developing employment is important for our municipality, but we must cooperate well with neighboring municipalities in this.

229816.explanation: We want to be careful with our landscape appearance and want to prevent large distribution centers from dominating our landscape.

229817.explanation: We believe that people should be guided to work as quickly as possible. The sooner this happens, the better; then people can integrate faster.

229818.explanation: We want to reduce CO2 emissions but not unnecessarily burden people with costs.

229820.explanation: Our preference is for solar panels on the roof.

229821.explanation: We do want decent spatial planning; where possible, opportunities should be available.

229822.explanation: The housing plan for schools provides sufficient space for new construction, maintenance, and improvement of school buildings. This does not necessarily have to apply to all schools.

229824.explanation: Considerable investments have been made here in recent years; we would like to first await the effects.

229825.explanation: We want to prevent enforcement from provoking aggression.

229826.explanation: We see an increasing demand for social rental housing and affordable home ownership; there is a need for this in Coevorden.

229827.explanation: Houses are not for speculation but to live in.

229829.explanation: There are enough parking spaces; two parking locations have been set up. Research shows this is sufficient.

229831.explanation: A loading vision was developed in the past administrative period. It meets the need.

229832.explanation: We believe logical connections between villages must remain. This is a provincial task.

229833.explanation: This aligns with our views on safety and mobility; 30 kilometers per hour contributes to that.

229836.explanation: We believe good spatial planning is important, but if we can reduce regulatory pressure, we are highly in favor of that.

229837.explanation: We believe there must be owner-occupancy.

229838.explanation: The municipality must supervise compliance with the good landlordship law.

229839.explanation: We cannot build everywhere all the time, but we can certainly make targeted choices that fit the character of our villages.

229841.explanation: The need is decisive; at the moment there seems to be a particular demand for homes for one- or two-person households.

229842.explanation: We are not in favor of bulb cultivation. The use of chemicals poses a risk to health.

229843.explanation: We no longer have space for this industrial class; it fits better within the Emmen industrial cluster.

229845.explanation: A protocol has been established by the province of Drenthe on how to deal with problem wolves.

229847.explanation: Successful car-sharing projects have not yet been realized in Coevorden.

229848.explanation: We have arranged this via the Doe-Mee-Web-Pas.

229849.explanation: In our opinion, the priority should be the Emmen-Gieten road section.

229850.explanation: The builder is responsible for parking spaces on the site.

229851.explanation: We support local ownership and control. Residents should be able to benefit from the proceeds; for large projects, achieving 50% may not always be feasible.

229852.explanation: We support owner-occupation; owning multiple houses should be unattractive to stimulate turnover in the housing stock.

229853.explanation: We consider accessibility very important but believe that entrepreneurs themselves are responsible for this.

229854.explanation: We consider respite care important; temporary accommodation elsewhere is necessary.

229855.explanation: We find it important that technology can be used to enable people to live at home longer. Budget for this has now been arranged through the regional deal.

229856.explanation: We believe providing guidance in the child’s own environment is important to prevent long-term problems.

229859.explanation: For veterinary costs, there are provisions such as assistance from animal welfare organizations that people can rely on.

229861.explanation: We more than meet the applicable standard. We are an inclusive municipality. But it can still improve.

229862.explanation: For that we have a Fixbus and a Doe-Mee-Webwinkel.

229863.explanation: The technology is still insufficiently reliable; we prefer personal contact.

229865.explanation: We support citizen panels, but we do question whether this is sufficiently safe and accessible digitally for everyone.

229868.explanation: At this moment, refugees do not have priority but there are fixed agreements with housing corporations that apply and which we want to maintain.

229869.explanation: This is also the agreement with the COA (Central Agency for the Reception of Asylum Seekers).

229870.explanation: The subsidy amount has been significantly increased during the past administrative period.

229871.explanation: We have made decisions about this. This location offers sufficient new perspectives for multiple initiatives. The majority of the land is owned by the municipality.

229873.explanation: The municipality must continue to sufficiently facilitate sports, with the needs of neighborhoods and villages leading.

229813.explanation: We support the lowest possible municipal taxes and charges and a sound financial policy. Money not used by the municipality should be returned to the residents.

229815.explanation: We support expansion of business parks such as Holsloot-Veenoord, Geesbrug, and Holwert-Midden. Additionally, existing business parks should be modernized.

229816.explanation: We see logistics and transport as a key sector. Depending on size and environmental impact, a distribution center fits within this.

229817.explanation: Integration must primarily come through work and personal responsibility, not through extra government spending. Empower people.

229818.explanation: In rural municipalities like ours, these kinds of freedoms must remain.

229820.explanation: We already have more than enough solar fields. The emphasis should be on solar panels on roofs of homes and businesses.

229821.explanation: However, this must be done carefully and with policy, so that construction does not happen haphazardly everywhere.

229822.explanation: We invest purposefully (for example Nieuwe Veste and education-business connections), but not indiscriminately in "all" buildings. Money must be spent efficiently.

229824.explanation: Additional community enforcement officers increase the visibility of enforcement, work preventively, and help combat nuisance and crime, making residents feel safer.

229825.explanation: The community enforcement officers must have the right tools to be able to act decisively.

229826.explanation: There must be a proportional distribution of housing types, so a good mix of social rental homes, affordable owner-occupied and rental homes, as well as homes in the higher segment.

229827.explanation: People should be able to determine for themselves as much as possible what they do with their home within the applicable regulations.

229829.explanation: It is important for the viability of the city center that there are enough parking spaces. Our shops and other facilities in the city center must be easily accessible.

229831.explanation: The number of electric cars is increasing sharply, so there must also be sufficient charging points.

229832.explanation: Research innovative solutions that visibly contribute to the mobility of our residents and are viable. This does not necessarily have to be tailored transport. People in need of assistance and the elderly already have access to various facilities.

229833.explanation: Not every built-up area in our municipality is suitable for this. Traffic research must provide clarity on where this is desirable.

229836.explanation: We are in favor of abolishing the aesthetic committee, but there will always be rules related to, for example, a protected village view or similar.

229837.explanation: We believe this solves a problem that hardly or does not at all exist in our municipality. It is patronizing and unenforceable.

229838.explanation: We see no need for stricter regulations and enforcement. Current legislation provides sufficiently for this.

229841.explanation: A healthy municipality requires a varied housing supply.

229842.explanation: Entrepreneurs use permissible crop protection agents. Municipalities should not impose additional rules beyond national regulations.

229843.explanation: Heavy industry that meets environmental standards and provides employment is welcome in Coevorden.

229845.explanation: As far as no other options are available.

229847.explanation: The parking supply within our municipality is adequate. No exceptions need to be made for this group.

229848.explanation: There are already various schemes that people with low incomes can apply for.

229849.explanation: For economic development but also for traffic safety, it is necessary to widen the N34. The traffic on this road has increased greatly in recent years and regularly leads to dangerous situations.

229850.explanation: The initiator of the construction must provide sufficient parking spaces, not the municipality.

229851.explanation: We do not want further expansion of wind turbines and solar parks. Valuable agricultural land is lost as a result.

229852.explanation: The VVD makes no distinction between people with one or multiple houses in Coevorden.

229853.explanation: Entrepreneurs are aware of their customer focus and will provide for this themselves.

229854.explanation: Investing in places to stay temporarily to give informal caregivers a break is not a municipal task. However, if initiatives come from society, the municipality can play a supportive (non-financial) role.

229855.explanation: We favor a personal approach and want to minimize the distance to the elderly. Using only technology actually increases this distance between the elderly and the municipality, which is undesirable.

229856.explanation: This prevents young people from unnecessarily ending up in youth care.

229859.explanation: Having a pet entails responsibilities, including paying the costs of a veterinarian.

229861.explanation: The municipality already pursues an active policy on this point.

229862.explanation: There are already various national subsidy schemes in this area.

229863.explanation: This fits with an efficient, modern, and service-oriented government.

229865.explanation: We want more participation and to involve residents in important decisions.

229868.explanation: The allocation of a house should be related to the urgency of the applicant.

229869.explanation: We believe there should be a maximum of one asylum seekers center in our municipality with a maximum number of 450 asylum seekers.

229870.explanation: Theater Hofpoort is a valuable cultural facility for our entire municipality. We will continue investing in the preservation and further development of this beautiful theater.

229871.explanation: We are in favor of the development and new construction of the Nieuwe Veste on Holwert Midden.

229873.explanation: We believe sufficient sports facilities are necessary to enable sports participation.

229813.explanation: The CDA wants to keep the property tax (OZB) as low as possible. It directly affects the purchasing power of families and households. The OZB should not be a budget balancing item or a patch for government cuts.

229815.explanation: We focus on developing the business park along the A37 near Geesbrug. Moreover, we find it important that local entrepreneurs have opportunities to run their business in or near their own community.

229816.explanation: Space on business parks is scarce, so we must use it sparingly. We believe it is important that new businesses create employment.

229817.explanation: Regarding newcomers, we see inclusion and integration as processes in which newcomers actively participate in society. Those who live or work here belong and contribute. As far as we are concerned, this does not mean spending more municipal money on newcomers' integration.

229818.explanation: A ban on the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves generates much regulatory burden and is hard to enforce. The CDA wants to reduce regulatory pressure in society.

229820.explanation: Space is scarce, also in our municipality. We are therefore not in favor of good agricultural land being easily exchanged for solar parks. At the same time, it is necessary to make our energy consumption sustainable. This therefore requires clear frameworks and responsible choices.

229821.explanation: The CDA wants to tackle the housing shortage, including on agricultural land if it does not hinder other businesses and fits within our beautiful rural area. Farmers who choose to end their business should, according to the CDA, have more opportunities to build homes in exchange for demolishing buildings.

229822.explanation: The CDA supports the education housing plan based on strong buildings. Together with schools, a good start has been made on new educational housing during the past council period, and we want to continue this. Good housing is a necessary condition for quality inclusive education.

229824.explanation: The CDA wants to increase safety in our municipality. More community enforcement officers contribute to this.

229825.explanation: The CDA believes that enforcers and emergency services must be able to perform their work safely and responsibly. We are concerned about violence against aid workers. Whether and what kind of means of force enforcers may use is a decision that should be coordinated nationally.

229826.explanation: The CDA believes there should be a good mix of affordable owner-occupied and rental homes in all segments. The Housing Act stipulates that 30% of new construction must be social housing. The local CDA wants to comply with the laws.

229827.explanation: The CDA finds it important that young people can continue to live in our municipality. For many residents, finding housing here has become more difficult. We support measures to assist them, such as an owner-occupancy obligation.

229829.explanation: The CDA believes that more parking spaces should have been preserved. In the evaluation of the parking policy, we aim to double the number of parking spaces at the market in the city center so that the town hall and shops remain as accessible as possible.

229831.explanation: The CDA considers it important to have sufficient charging points for electric cars. These should be placed based on demand, where people with electric cars live and work. Residents should be able to submit an application to the municipality for this.

229832.explanation: Good connections with public transport are important to the CDA. We therefore support the Nedersaksen line. For bus transport, we want the current bus lines to remain intact at least. People who depend on public transport should be able to use hub taxis.

229833.explanation: The CDA wants you to be able to safely drive at 50 km/h on main roads in the built-up area. The standard in residential streets is 30 km/h.

229836.explanation: We want to reduce the number of rules, including those related to building aesthetics. Completely abolishing rules for building and renovating is undesirable because the coherence in neighborhoods and the value of surrounding homes can be diminished if everyone can determine how their own house looks.

229837.explanation: The CDA is not in favor of unlimited rental of homes. In times of housing shortage, houses must primarily remain available for residents.

229838.explanation: The CDA finds it important that landlords adhere to the rules, especially in times of housing shortage. We support smart and effective enforcement, but with attention to the human dimension.

229839.explanation: The CDA considers it important that residents can have a say in policy and that there is room for local initiatives.

229841.explanation: The CDA believes that square meters of living space should be used as efficiently as possible. It is not necessary to build more large owner-occupied and rental homes. The housing stock already contains enough large homes to accommodate large families.

229842.explanation: In Drenthe, all bulb growers have signed the Drentse Bulb Cultivation covenant. Within two years, the use of crop protection products in bulb cultivation will be equal to that used for potatoes, for example. Mutual consultation and information sharing deliver results faster than a ban.

229843.explanation: Within the municipality of Coevorden, the focus is on innovation and manufacturing industry: The municipality works together with neighboring municipalities in the Dutch TechZone to promote smart and sustainable manufacturing. The CDA supports this.

229845.explanation: CDA Coevorden aims for active management of the wolf and population control. If a wolf causes problems, the municipality must urge the province to have it shot. We will certainly actively approach our party colleagues in Assen for this.

229847.explanation: If it turns out there is demand for shared cars and the concept works in practice, CDA Coevorden supports designating more parking spaces for shared cars.

229848.explanation: CDA Coevorden considers it important that everyone can participate in our society. Therefore, we are positive about additional support for residents with low incomes, for example through the Doe mee web store. Travel compensation could also be part of this.

229849.explanation: The CDA values good accessibility. If it appears that the N34 between Coevorden and Hardenberg will become a bottleneck, then the municipality of Coevorden should request widening from the province of Overijssel.

229850.explanation: For new homes, it is important that there are sufficient parking spaces. The municipality does not need to provide extra parking spaces; that is the responsibility of the initiator.

229851.explanation: The CDA believes residents should be able to benefit from wind turbines or solar parks in their immediate vicinity. The goal of 50 percent ownership is part of this.

229852.explanation: Coevorden has a relatively low commuter tax. CDA Coevorden believes this tax can be increased. The CDA supports permanent occupancy of homes to combat the housing shortage.

229853.explanation: The municipality has a task to make public spaces accessible. The CDA is willing to allocate funds for this. Keeping store entrances accessible is the responsibility of the entrepreneurs themselves.

229854.explanation: The CDA advocates for more support for informal caregivers. An example is funding respite care.

229855.explanation: CDA Coevorden believes technology is a supplement, not an obligation, and that the choice remains with the elderly themselves. Technology and remote care can also help alleviate informal caregivers somewhat.

229856.explanation: Schools are a good place to help parents more preventively. Youth counselors can play an important role in improving collaboration between education and youth care. CDA Coevorden thinks it is important not to treat all behavior as a problem. A child should be allowed to be a child.

229859.explanation: CDA Coevorden finds it important that pets do not suffer due to financial problems of their owners. Through special assistance, the municipality could do something in exceptional cases, but we do not believe the municipality should routinely pay all veterinary costs.

229861.explanation: CDA Coevorden supports the ambition to employ more people with disabilities. Everyone deserves the chance to participate. The municipality must set a good example with real jobs, good support, and attention to what people can do.

229862.explanation: The Fixteam Drenthe helps people for free to take energy-saving measures. Coevorden also has a subsidy scheme for residents with little money to insulate their homes. The CDA supports this.

229863.explanation: The CDA attaches great value to a personal approach and good service. AI can be a tool, but people must always have the option to speak to an employee, especially for complex or sensitive matters.

229865.explanation: CDA Coevorden would like to involve more residents in thinking about important issues. Input from society is very important for making good choices. A digital citizen panel could be a good tool for this.

229868.explanation: Every municipality is required to accommodate a number of status holders. CDA Coevorden believes that in the current housing shortage, the priority for status holders should be better balanced against other groups who also have priority when finding housing.

229869.explanation: The asylum seekers center in Aalden has space for and agreements about a maximum of 450 asylum seekers. In case of a desire to temporarily exceed this number in emergencies, explicit consideration must also be given to other locations within the municipality.

229870.explanation: CDA Coevorden supports spending money on a cultural incubator as a complement to Theater Hofpoort. In such an incubator, everyone can participate, learn, and create. This helps talent grow and keeps culture alive in the city and villages.

229871.explanation: The municipal council has decided to move the school to Holwert-Midden because this location offers the best guarantees for future-oriented and accessible secondary education within the municipality. This location brings extra challenges but also additional opportunities.

229873.explanation: The CDA supports good consultation with residents, entrepreneurs, and associations. This also applies when a sports field disappears and a new location must be jointly sought.

statements.title: The tax for people with a second home in Coevorden should be increased.
No more than 450 people should ever be accommodated in the asylum seekers' center (AZC).

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

titleParts.text: (azc) mogen nooit meer dan 450 mensen worden opgevangen.

statements.moreInfo: Until June last year, the asylum seekers’ centre (azc) in Aalden housed 500 people. The municipality had temporarily given permission for this. This number was lowered to 450 by a decision of the municipal council. There is a large shortage of accommodation places for asylum seekers throughout the Netherlands.

statements.title: The municipality should invest more in places where people who are cared for at home by informal carers can stay temporarily.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld steken in plekken waar mensen die thuis worden verzorgd door

titleParts.glossary: Someone who provides unpaid and often long-term care for sick family members or friends.

statements.title: The municipality should ban the use of agricultural pesticides in lily cultivation around schools, homes and sports fields.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het gebruik van landbouwgif in de
verbieden rond scholen, woningen en sportvelden.

statements.title: Property tax (ozb) must not be increased.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should limit the number of days people are allowed to rent out their homes.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should give more money to Theater Hofpoort in Coevorden.
The municipality does not need to provide extra parking spaces for new housing.
Coevorden should get a digital citizens' panel where residents can give their opinions on important issues.
Coevorden should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Each village may decide for itself whether it wants to grow with new homes. The municipality will discuss this with residents.
Every school should have youth counselors who help children and parents with problems.
statements.moreInfo: A youth counselor helps children, young people and families with problems. Youth counselors are employed by the municipality. They help with, for example, parenting problems or psychological complaints. They can also help with high-conflict divorces or domestic violence. In addition, they assist with learning problems at school or truancy.

statements.title: Low-income people should be able to ride the bus for free. The municipality should pay for that.
The municipality should look for another location for the new school building of De Nieuwe Veste.
statements.moreInfo: In 2023 the municipal council decided that the secondary school De Nieuwe Veste will get a new school building. The new building is to be located behind the train station in Coevorden, on the Holwert-Midden business park. For that the municipality must buy parcels of land, but one entrepreneur does not want to sell his land. That is why there is still a lawsuit.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the city center of Coevorden.
At least 30% of the newly built homes in Coevorden must be social housing. In Coevorden, 23% of all homes are currently social housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Coevorden moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Coevorden is nu 23% van alle woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: If a wolf causes problems, the municipality must urge the province to have it shot.
The municipality must give businesses money to make their shop entrances wheelchair accessible.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality must pay veterinary costs for people with low incomes who have a pet.
If a sports field disappears, the municipality must designate another location for a new sports field.
The municipality must invest in tailored transport between villages, for example a community bus.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in

titleParts.glossary: A type of public transport that doesn't run on a fixed schedule but can be called to pick you up.
A small bus that runs in villages or neighbourhoods with little public transport.

statements.title: The municipality must use technology to help elderly people with care needs live at home, such as remote care via a screen.
The municipality must employ more people with disabilities.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The municipality must not give refugees with residence permits priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Residents with low incomes must receive free insulation materials for their homes from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Inwoners met weinig geld moeten van de gemeente gratis

titleParts.glossary: Material in roofs, walls and floors that keeps heat in or out. In winter it keeps heat inside and in summer outside.

statements.title: More large owner-occupied and rental homes must be built in Coevorden.
The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

statements.title: The municipality must ask the province to widen the N34 between Hardenberg and Coevorden from one to two lanes.

shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should spend more money on integrating newcomers into society.
More business parks should be developed in Coevorden.
Anyone who buys a home in our municipality must live in it themselves.
New wind turbine or solar panel projects should be at least 50 percent owned by residents of the municipality.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.
In Coevorden the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.
The municipality should more strictly enforce that homeowners comply with rental rules.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces for car-sharing vehicles.
The municipality should abolish rules about how houses must look during construction and renovation.
The municipality should not grant permission for new heavy industry.
The municipality should have simple questions at the digital service desk answered by artificial intelligence (AI).
The enforcement officers (BOAs) who monitor safety in Coevorden should be given a baton.
